question: List the names of employees in the Engineering department.
schema_text:
CREATE TABLE departments (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL);
CREATE TABLE employees (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, department_id INTEGER NOT NULL, salary INTEGER NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY (department_id) REFERENCES departments(id));
target_sql: SELECT employees.name FROM employees JOIN departments ON employees.department_id = departments.id WHERE departments.name = 'Engineering';

question: What is the average salary for each department?
schema_text:
CREATE TABLE departments (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL);
CREATE TABLE employees (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, department_id INTEGER NOT NULL, salary INTEGER NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY (department_id) REFERENCES departments(id));
target_sql: SELECT departments.name, AVG(employees.salary) AS average_salary FROM departments JOIN employees ON employees.department_id = departments.id GROUP BY departments.name;

question: Show the names of employees who earn more than 100000.
schema_text:
CREATE TABLE departments (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL);
CREATE TABLE employees (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, department_id INTEGER NOT NULL, salary INTEGER NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY (department_id) REFERENCES departments(id));
target_sql: SELECT name FROM employees WHERE salary > 100000;

question: Count employees in each department.
schema_text:
CREATE TABLE departments (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL);
CREATE TABLE employees (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, department_id INTEGER NOT NULL, salary INTEGER NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY (department_id) REFERENCES departments(id));
target_sql: SELECT departments.name, COUNT(employees.id) AS employee_count FROM departments LEFT JOIN employees ON employees.department_id = departments.id GROUP BY departments.name;

question: List each employee name with their department name.
schema_text:
CREATE TABLE departments (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL);
CREATE TABLE employees (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, department_id INTEGER NOT NULL, salary INTEGER NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY (department_id) REFERENCES departments(id));
target_sql: SELECT employees.name, departments.name FROM employees JOIN departments ON employees.department_id = departments.id;
